Interface Physical2D

All Known Subinterfaces:
PhysicalRenderable2D
All Known Implementing Classes:
Sprite
public interface Physical2D
A physical 2-dimensional object with properties like world coordinates, direction, X/Y axis stretching, and a hitbox. These properties are anchored to the object's center point.

It's important to distinguish between screen coordinate system (SCS; origin in top-left corner; X axis right, Y axis down) vs. world coordinate system (WCS; origin in the center; X axis right, Y axis up). SCS is more convenient for rendering, while WCS is preferred for physics calculations. While it's possible to convert between them if bounds are known, they should NOT be mixed together to prevent confusions and sudden bugs.

Therefore, do not implement this interface if you want a purely graphical entity!

See Also:
  • PhysicalRenderable2D

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    double
    getDirection()
    Returns the current direction in degrees.
    Hitbox2D
    getHitbox()
    Returns this object's collision box, as if without any transformations applied.
    RotationMode
    getRotationMode()
    Returns the rotation mode.
    double
    getStretchX()
    Returns the X axis stretch in percents.
    double
    getStretchY()
    Returns the Y axis stretch in percents.
    double
    getX()
    Returns the current X coordinate.
    double
    getY()
    Returns the current Y coordinate.

  • Method Details

    • getX

      double getX()
      Returns the current X coordinate.
      Returns:
      the current X coordinate

    • getY

      double getY()
      Returns the current Y coordinate.
      Returns:
      the current Y coordinate

    • getDirection

      double getDirection()
      Returns the current direction in degrees. The direction is relative to the upper vertical axis: 0 means facing up, 90 means no rotation.
      Returns:
      the current direction in degrees

    • getRotationMode

      RotationMode getRotationMode()
      Returns the rotation mode. The returned value changes how this object gets rendered depending on the direction.
      Returns:
      the rotation mode
      See Also:
      • RotationMode

    • getStretchX

      double getStretchX()
      Returns the X axis stretch in percents. If you don't want to apply stretch, return 100 (100% stretch - no change).
      Returns:
      the X axis stretch in percents

    • getStretchY

      double getStretchY()
      Returns the Y axis stretch in percents. If you don't want to apply stretch, return 100 (100% stretch - no change).
      Returns:
      the Y axis stretch in percents

    • getHitbox

      Hitbox2D getHitbox()
      Returns this object's collision box, as if without any transformations applied.
      Returns:
      the collision box
